374.reason: [Incorrectly generates an AND token for a single '&' character. According to the specification, a single '&' should be treated as an error and should not produce a valid token. The lexer should only report the error without adding any token to the stream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly generates an AND token for a single '&' character. According to the specification, a single '&' should be treated as an error and should not produce a valid token. The lexer should only report the error without adding any token to the stream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly generates AND token for single '&' which should be illegal; should not produce any valid token and only report error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly generates a valid AND token for a single '&', which should be treated as an error without producing a token]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly assigns TokenType.AND to a single '&' character; should not create a valid token for illegal single '&' and should only record the error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Should not generate an AND token for a single '&'; this incorrectly treats illegal standalone '&' as a valid AND token before reporting the error]

385.reason: [Incorrectly generates an OR token for a single '|' character. According to the specification, a single '|' should be treated as an error and should not produce a valid token. The lexer should only report the error without adding any token to the stream.]

377.reason: [Error: ungetc() should be called before adding the error token, not after; the current character 'c' is the one following '&', so we need to push it back to be processed later, but we shouldn't have consumed it as part of an AND token]
